objective: Confirm that a user can create a payment exception case
preconditions: User has access to the exception workflow
test_steps: Open the workflow; select Create Case; enter required payment details; save the case
expected_result: A unique case ID is generated and the case is created with status New

objective: Confirm that mandatory payment and exception fields are enforced
preconditions: User has access to create a case
test_data: Case with missing payment reference and currency
test_steps: Open Create Case; leave mandatory fields blank; attempt to continue or save
expected_result: The workflow prevents progression and identifies the missing mandatory fields

test_case_name: Select exception type from controlled list
objective: Confirm that only approved exception types can be selected
preconditions: A new or active case exists
test_steps: Open the exception type field; review available values; select Bank Rejection; save
expected_result: Only controlled exception types are available and the selected value is saved

test_case_name: Move case from New to In Review
objective: Confirm that a valid status transition can be completed
preconditions: A complete case exists in status New
test_steps: Open the case; select status In Review; save changes
expected_result: The status changes from New to In Review and the change is recorded

test_case_name: Prevent direct transition from New to Closed
objective: Confirm that an invalid direct closure is prevented
preconditions: A case exists in status New
test_steps: Open the case; attempt to select Closed; attempt to save
expected_result: The workflow prevents the transition from New directly to Closed

objective: Confirm that a case can be assigned to an analyst
preconditions: A complete open case exists
test_steps: Open the case; select an owner; save changes
expected_result: The selected owner is stored and displayed in the case and exception queue

objective: Confirm that team leads can identify unassigned open cases
preconditions: At least one open unassigned case exists
test_data: Case with no owner and no team queue
test_steps: Open the Team Lead Control View; filter for unassigned cases
expected_result: The unassigned case is displayed and can be opened for assignment

test_case_name: Assign High priority to high-value case
objective: Confirm that high-value cases receive an appropriate priority
preconditions: A payment exception meets the high-value threshold
test_steps: Create or open the high-value case; review priority and high-value flag
expected_result: The case is flagged as high value and has priority High or Critical

objective: Confirm that duplicate payment risk receives appropriate review
preconditions: A potential duplicate payment has been identified
test_data: Two payment instructions with matching business details
test_steps: Create the case; select Duplicate Payment Risk; save and review escalation indicators
expected_result: The duplicate risk flag is recorded and the case has High or Critical priority

objective: Confirm that the SLA due date is calculated from priority and creation date
preconditions: A complete open case exists
test_data: Medium priority case created on a business day
test_steps: Create the case; assign Medium priority; save; review SLA due date
expected_result: The correct SLA due date is calculated according to the defined business rules

objective: Confirm that an overdue open case is identified as SLA breached
preconditions: An open case exists beyond its SLA due date
test_data: Open case with SLA due date before the reporting date
test_steps: Open the case and SLA report; review the SLA breach indicator
expected_result: The SLA breach flag is Yes and the case appears in the SLA breach report

objective: Confirm that open cases are placed in the correct aging bucket
preconditions: An open case exists with a known created date
test_steps: Open the case or aged report; review days open and aging bucket
expected_result: The case shows 8 days open and appears in the 6-10 days bucket

objective: Confirm that cases open over five business days are flagged as aged
preconditions: An open case exists for more than five business days
test_steps: Open the case and aged exceptions report; review the aged flag
expected_result: The aged exception flag is Yes and the case appears in the aged report

objective: Confirm that a high-risk overdue case can be escalated
preconditions: A High priority case has breached SLA
test_data: High priority bank rejection with overdue SLA
test_steps: Open the case; select Escalate; enter escalation reason and owner; save
expected_result: The case is escalated and escalation details are recorded in case history

objective: Confirm that investigation activity can be documented
preconditions: An active case exists
test_steps: Open the case; add an investigation note; save the note
expected_result: The note is stored with author and timestamp and is visible in case history

objective: Confirm that a case cannot close without investigation evidence
preconditions: A case exists in status Resolved with no investigation notes
test_steps: Open the case; select a closure reason; attempt to close
expected_result: The workflow prevents closure and requests investigation notes

objective: Confirm that a case cannot close without a closure reason
preconditions: A case exists in status Resolved with investigation notes
test_data: Resolved case with no closure reason
test_steps: Open the case; attempt to close without selecting a closure reason
expected_result: The workflow prevents closure and requests a closure reason

objective: Confirm that an eligible Resolved case can be closed
preconditions: A case is Resolved and contains notes and closure reason
test_data: Resolved case with complete closure evidence
test_steps: Open the case; verify mandatory closure fields; select Close Case
expected_result: The status changes to Closed and the closed date and closing user are recorded

objective: Confirm that material case changes are retained
preconditions: An active case has multiple recorded changes
test_data: Case with status owner priority escalation and note updates
test_steps: Open the case; navigate to Case History; review recorded events
expected_result: The history displays chronological changes with timestamp user previous value and new value

objective: Confirm that users can filter the exception queue
preconditions: Multiple cases exist with different attributes
test_data: Open cases across statuses owners and priorities
test_steps: Open the queue; apply status priority owner type channel and SLA filters
expected_result: Only cases matching all selected filters are displayed

objective: Confirm that the SLA breach report shows all breached open cases
preconditions: Open SLA-breached and non-breached cases exist
test_steps: Open the SLA Breach Report; compare results with source cases
expected_result: All and only open SLA-breached cases are displayed with required fields

objective: Confirm that aged cases are reported by aging bucket
preconditions: Open cases exist across multiple age ranges
test_steps: Open the Aged Exceptions Report; review case counts and buckets
expected_result: Aged open cases are displayed in the correct aging buckets

objective: Confirm that escalated cases and escalation details are visible
preconditions: Escalated and non-escalated cases exist
test_steps: Open the Escalation Report; review case reason owner and date
expected_result: Escalated cases are displayed with complete escalation information

objective: Confirm that workload is summarized by owner and queue
preconditions: Open cases are assigned across multiple owners
test_steps: Open the Owner Workload Report; compare counts with the exception queue
expected_result: Open case counts and risk indicators are correctly grouped by owner and queue

objective: Confirm that management KPIs and breakdowns are accurate
preconditions: Sample data is available for the selected reporting period
test_steps: Open the Management Summary; apply reporting period; compare KPIs with source records
expected_result: Open aged breached escalated new and closed case metrics match the underlying data

objective: Confirm that key control exceptions are visible to reviewers
preconditions: Sample data includes control-compliant and control-failure scenarios
test_steps: Open the Control Evidence Report; review each key control indicator
expected_result: The report identifies ownership SLA aging closure escalation and data quality control results

objective: Confirm that invalid status values cannot be stored
preconditions: A case exists and status can be updated
test_steps: Open the status field or import process; attempt to use an invalid status
expected_result: The invalid status is rejected or flagged by data quality validation

objective: Confirm that invalid priority values cannot be stored
preconditions: A case exists and priority can be updated
test_steps: Open the priority field or import process; attempt to use an invalid priority
expected_result: The invalid priority is rejected or flagged by data quality validation

objective: Confirm that closed date cannot be earlier than created date
preconditions: A case exists with a known created date
test_data: Created date 2025-02-10 and attempted closed date 2025-02-09
test_steps: Attempt to close or import the case using a closed date before the created date
expected_result: The invalid date sequence is rejected or included in the data quality report

objective: Confirm that filtered queue data can be exported
preconditions: The exception queue contains data and filters are available
test_steps: Apply filters; select Export CSV; open the generated file
expected_result: The CSV contains the expected filtered records and required columns
